description_long: This object consists of a tightly packed rectangular array of identical clear plastic water bottles, each with a blue screw cap. The bottles are arranged in rows and columns, forming a flat-topped block with the caps all facing upward. The bottles are cylindrical with a slight taper toward the neck, and the blue caps are small and rounded. The plastic material is translucent, allowing some visibility of the contents inside, and the bottles appear to be filled with clear liquid. There are no visible labels or branding on the bottles, giving them a generic appearance. The overall structure resembles a shrink-wrapped multipack of bottled water, commonly found in urban retail or storage settings.
description: A rectangular multipack of clear plastic water bottles with blue caps, arranged in a tight grid and filled with clear liquid. The bottles are unbranded and have a translucent appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the row of blue-capped bottles aligned side by side, highlighting the rectangular arrangement.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the narrow profile of the multipack, with the blue caps forming a straight line along the top.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the back row of bottles and the uniformity of the arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and the alignment of the blue caps.
attribute_cap_type: screw cap; small, rounded, blue plastic with ridges for grip
attribute_transparency: translucent; allows partial visibility of the liquid inside, with no patterns or gradients
attribute_bottle_neck_design: short, slightly tapered neck with visible threading for the screw cap; no flares or unique features
